title: Citrusy Tender Ukrainian Chicken Soup
description: A refreshing, whole30-friendly soup bursting with citrus and apricot sweetness, perfect for easing a hangover.
instructions: Rinse the chicken thighs and pat dry. Place them in a stockpot, cover with water, and bring to a gentle boil. Swirl to loosen impurities, blanch for 2 minutes, then transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in the same pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrots, celery, and minced garlic. Sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the dried thyme, then pour in the low-sodium chicken broth. Bring to a rolling boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Return the blanched chicken thighs to the pot. Stir in the apricot jam and mix well.
Simmer uncovered for 25–30 minutes, or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender.
Remove the chicken from the pot. Using two forks, shred the meat, discarding bones or skin.
Return shredded chicken to the pot. Stir in the lemon zest and juice, orange zest and juice, salt, and pepper.
Taste the soup; adjust seasoning if needed. Let it simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Ladle the soup into bowls, garnish with fresh dill if desired, and serve hot.
